rulings.Case Title: NEW HORIZON TRUST ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained to the first cause of action for writ a writ of mandate with leave to amend for Plaintiff to state a claim regarding the purported violation of HAA and specify an application other than SB-35 application. Demurrer to the first cause of action for writ of mandate is sustained without leave to amend to the extent it is based on the purported misinterpretation within HCD guidelines followed by the City. Petitioner failed to cite any authority for the proposition that the City may disregard these guidelines in favor of Plaintiff's interpretation of SB-35. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is overruled. While the cause of action for writ of mandate and declaratory relief may overlap, the cause of action for declaratory relief is not entirely derivative, e.g. the alleged policy detailed in Planning Director Bulletin 5 and SB-35 application form. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JIE YING LI ET AL VS. CRAIG S. CHIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2024 line 2. 1-DEFENDANT CAROLYN CHIN, STEPHANIE CHAN, SIMON WONG, CRAIG CHAN, HOGAN & VEST, INC.'s DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. OVERRULED: (1) the 4th cause of action is expressly not alleged against the moving defendants as is stated in the title of the cause of action and confirmed in opposition; (2) the grounds for demurrer to the 8th cause of action appeared on the fact of the FAC barring this demurrer. Both grounds for the demurrer listed in the Notice of Motion could have been raised on a demurrer to the First Amended Complaint and the amendments made to this cause of action did not create the claimed deficiency. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ARKADY BERGER ET AL VS. DAVID DEDIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 1, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID DEDIA, OLGA DEDIA MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and only as to the activity "that supplies one or more elements of plaintiff's claims." Specifically, the allegations regarding the mechanic's lien forming the basis of the claims for breach of contract, slander of title, and UCL violation and not the filing of the bankruptcy or background facts. RGC Gaslamp LLC v. Ehmcke Sheet Metal, Co (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 413. Therefore, the following shall be stricken: (1) the cause of action for slander of title based in its entirety on a claim arising out of a mechanic's lien; (2) "the recordation of wrongful mechanics' lien claim against 23-23A Wood," in paragraph 43; (3) "Defendants subsequent misuse of the Mechanics' lien remedy by the subsequent recordation of a meritless Mechanics' Lien Claim, Defendants' refusal to remove their unenforceable Mechanics' lien Claims for a period of two years" in paragraph 73; (4) "by casting a cloud on title, ·with the recordation of meritless mechanics' lien claims" in paragraph 74. The Court notes that Plaintiffs failed to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to remove the mechanic's lien (recordation of which Plaintiffs concede is protected activity) is legally distinguishable and is not protected/privileged. The Plaintiff further failed to show the merits of their case by establishing facts that would, if accepted, negate the litigation privilege prerequisites. Defendant's unopposed request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $9,280. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
